title: Workflow Profile
description: Declarative workflow profile consumed by the /team interpreter. One input (classification) selects exactly one profile; the interpreter walks `stages` in order, reading/writing typed artifacts under .work-state/artifacts/. See workflows/README.md.

match.description: Classification selector. A profile is chosen when classification.type is in `type` AND (complexity is in `complexity` OR complexity is omitted). The first profile (in selection order) whose match passes wins. See workflows/README.md for the resolution table.

id.description: Unique stage id within the profile. Used as stage_cursor in team-state.json.

type.description: orchestrator = main context performs it (no subagent). single = exactly one subagent (role resolved via team.config or scope). consilium = N subagents in parallel (see `roles`). bash = deterministic shell step, no model. none = placeholder/skip.

roles.description: For consilium: list of roles to launch in parallel. Role names resolve to agents via .claude/team.config.json `roles` (falling back to built-in defaults). A role may repeat (e.g. architect x3 for design options).

role.description: For single: the role to launch. May be a template like "${scope.dev_agent}" resolved from the scope of touched files.

parallel.description: For consilium: launch all roles in one message (true) or sequentially (false).

consumes.description: Artifact ids this stage reads from .work-state/artifacts/<id>.json before running. The interpreter loads them and threads relevant content into subagent prompts — replaces ad-hoc 'paste section'.

produces.description: Artifact id(s) this stage writes to .work-state/artifacts/<id>.json when complete. Schema per type in workflows/artifacts-schema.json. May be an array when a stage emits more than one (e.g. exploration also produces the dod).

checkpoint.description: Named user checkpoint. Interactive mode: stop and wait. Autonomous mode: skip + log the auto-decision (see `autonomous`).

autonomous.description: Auto-decision applied at this stage's checkpoint when running in autonomous mode (e.g. 'pick option #1', 'abort needs-human if confidence LOW').

gate.description: Precondition that must hold before the interpreter may mark this stage done (e.g. 'branch_created', 'confidence>=80', 'tests_passed').

conditional.description: Data-driven roster adjustments evaluated against scope flags — replaces 'EM decides which agents to launch'.

skip_if.description: Skip this stage when the expression is true (e.g. 'review.findings == []', 'autonomous').

loop.description: Optional bounded loop (e.g. debug cycle). The interpreter repeats `back_to` until `until` or `max_iterations`.

back_to.description: Stage id to loop back to on failure.
